messages.content:
Create a retrieval-oriented summary for this parent section of a document.
Use the child summaries below. Mention the topics, entities, dates, tables,
figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types covered
when they are visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

Parent:
{
  "node_id": "0116",
  "title": "休闲手游 投放趋势观察",
  "page_index": 61,
  "page_range": "61-62"
}

Child summaries:
[
  {
    "node_id": "0117",
    "title": "休闲手游 热门创意策略",
    "page_range": "62",
    "summary": "This section details popular creative strategies for casual mobile game advertising throughout 2025, focusing on AI-generated quirky openings, viral short video memes, and the adaptation of trending internet tropes. It outlines standard creative procedures, specifically the \"AI opening → actual gameplay → gameplay showcase\" and \"viral video opening → in-game recreation → gameplay showcase\" workflows. The analysis covers January to December 2025 and references entities including casual mobile games, popular mechanics (parkour, jumping, matching, water sorting), top puzzle genres (Mahjong connect, Sudoku, word puzzles, solitaire), and data platforms GuangdaDa and TikTok. Key claims assert that entertaining content has become the core standard for ad openings, future creatives will increasingly source directly from TikTok trends or creator videos, and video creatives significantly outperform other formats in impression share for leading puzzle games. Metrics are visualized in two monthly trend figures tracking advertiser volume (~7.0K–10.0K) and new creative launch rates (~76.9%–86.8%), alongside average active creatives (0–300) and new creative percentages (~43.4%–53.5%). Evidence is sourced from GuangdaDa’s Ad Creative and Advertiser Analysis modules, utilizing platform analytics, trend forecasting, and impression share benchmarking."
  }
]

message.content: This section (pages 61–62) analyzes casual mobile game advertising trends and creative strategies across January–December 2025. It outlines standard creative procedures, specifically the "AI opening → actual gameplay → gameplay showcase" and "viral video opening → in-game recreation → gameplay showcase" workflows. Key entities covered include casual mobile games, popular mechanics (parkour, jumping, matching, water sorting), top puzzle genres (Mahjong connect, Sudoku, word puzzles, solitaire), and data platforms GuangdaDa and TikTok. Core claims assert that entertaining content has become the baseline for ad openings, future creatives will increasingly source directly from TikTok trends or creator videos, and video formats significantly outperform others in impression share for leading puzzle games. Performance metrics are visualized in two monthly trend figures tracking advertiser volume (~7.0K–10.0K), new creative launch rates (~76.9%–86.8%), average active creatives (0–300), and new creative percentages (~43.4%–53.5%). Evidence is drawn from GuangdaDa’s Ad Creative and Advertiser Analysis modules, utilizing platform analytics, trend forecasting, and impression share benchmarking.

Core claims assert that entertaining content has become the baseline for ad openings, future creatives will increasingly source directly from TikTok trends or creator videos, and video formats significantly outperform others in impression share for leading puzzle games.
